rag_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid component type or implementation scopes, with assumptions/guarantees in type scopes and implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes
Assign statements in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use type scope for output dependency contracts
generic_rules: AGREE expressions must resolve to valid types; avoid <error> types by ensuring all identifiers are properly declared or visible
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes to prevent resolution errors
AGREE scalar types are int, real, and bool; avoid invalid type names like 'integer' or 'boolean'
When AGREE reports type mismatches, align helper declarations with expression types (bool for comparisons, int for integers, real for reals)
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Component implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings and assignments
Use only visible identifiers or declared helpers in AGREE annexes to avoid resolution errors
anti_patterns: Placing synchrony or assert statements outside of component implementations
Using unresolved references or invalid type names in AGREE declarations
Assigning to variables that are not outputs or declared auxiliary variables in implementation scope
Including implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE validator diagnostics about specific component names
Component type and implementation structure in AADL
